title: Trees, Branch by Branch
tagline: BSTs, heaps, traversals — build them, query them, watch them sift up and down
summary: The data structure that quietly powers ordered maps, priority queues, autocomplete, and half the algorithms textbook. Build a binary search tree by typing values, watch traversals walk through it in four different orders, then switch to a heap and see sift-up/sift-down preserve the heap invariant on every insert and extract.
highlights: Build a BST by typing values — see search find the matching node in O(log n) when the tree is balanced.
Animate all four traversals (pre-order, in-order, post-order, level-order) on the same tree.
Live min-heap and max-heap: insert sifts up, extract-min sifts down, both shown step by step.
Heapify a random array in O(n) and watch the bottom-up algorithm beat the obvious one.
Why balance matters: insert sorted values, get a linked list (O(n) search); insert in random order, get O(log n).
